blocks.content: The Invisible (6)

blocks.speaker: Maria
blocks.content: Whew. It's already midnight. Time flies.
Maria closed her book and stretched lazily.
The faint sound of the bell echoed outside.

blocks.speaker: Maria
blocks.content: Unconsciously, she recalled the presentation.
She shook her head quickly, trying to erase the thought.
But the more she tried to forget, the more it nagged at her.

blocks.speaker: Maria
blocks.content: Neid's words about the hungry ghosts appearing at midnight lingered in her mind.

blocks.speaker: Maria
blocks.content: Ugh, what's wrong with me? Why can't I stop thinking about it?
Shirone had anticipated this.
Once a keyword was planted, the imagination would take over, no further persuasion needed.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If you match the frequency of souls, you'll see ghosts?

blocks.speaker: Maria
blocks.content: Thoughts spiraled.
Maria shuddered and turned toward her bed.
At the same time, her heart plummeted.
Her pupils shook as an electric jolt shot up her spine.

blocks.speaker: Maria
blocks.content: Uh… uh…
A rotting corpse was staggering around her room.
Maria's jaw trembled uncontrollably.
The skeletal soldier turned toward her, raising its arms as it approached.

blocks.speaker: Maria
blocks.content: Kyaaaaaaah!
Her scream shook the windows.
But no one could hear her.
The same screams echoed through every hallway.
Crash!
A flower vase shattered against the wall.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: What the hell? What the hell?
Amy, who had thrown it, pressed her limbs against the opposite wall, panting heavily.
The moment the bell rang, the ghosts had appeared.
Was this the resonance Neid had mentioned?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: At first, she thought it was a hallucination.
But the figures before her were undeniably real.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Grrrr…
As the ghost let out a guttural moan, Amy activated her schema and bolted for the door.
She flung it open with enough force to break it and found girls screaming and running through the hallway.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Amy! Help me! Ghosts appeared!
Seriel cried, tears and snot streaming down her face.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Ugh, ugh!
Swept up in the panicked crowd, Amy began running toward the building's exit.
Grrrr…
A grotesque, transparent figure broke the silence of the secluded training hall.
Etela emerged from meditation.
She stood and turned to face the approaching specter.
Though the thought of a ghost crossed her mind, her ascetic heart felt no particular fear.
Squinting, Etela examined the ghost now inches from her face.
The ghost tilted its head, studying her in return.
It was proof of how precise Iruki's equations were.

blocks.speaker: Etela
blocks.content: Grrrr…

blocks.speaker: Etela
blocks.content: Grrrr?
Curious, Etela raised her hand and poked the soldier's nose.
Of course, her finger passed straight through to the back of its skull.
Haah! Haah!
In the frozen veil of steam, Siena clutched her naked body, her breath ragged.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: What in the world?
The first thing that came to mind was the soul's frequency.
Did that mean Neid's words were true? Impossible.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Grrrr…
The ghostly soldier phased through the ice barrier.
Strangely, this calmed Siena.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: How dare you…!
Siena strode forward confidently.
As expected, the ghost passed harmlessly through her.
Realization dawned.
She spotted two devices installed on the ceiling and walls.
Tiny, hemispherical units emitted thin beams of light.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: These little…!
Alpheus Magic School was in chaos.
Darkened windows lit up one after another as students screamed and spilled out of buildings.
The same scene played out everywhere.
The holograms, programmed to anticipate human behavior, moved freely, and the terrified students fled blindly, unaware they'd been tricked.

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: Aaaaah! Help!

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: Ghosts! Ghosts are here!
Human behavior under extreme fear was surprisingly predictable.
With rationality paralyzed, only instinct remained.
Lower-class students cried for their mothers, while upper-class students, separated by gender, merged into the largest crowds.
From the higher grounds, graduating-class students—mostly girls with a few boys mixed in—poured down like a flood.
The ghosts appeared to chase randomly, but from above, it was clear they were herding everyone toward one place.
Soon, nearly 400 students had gathered in the central park.
Trapped between people and buildings, the students finally realized: ghosts surrounded them on all sides, leaving no escape.

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: Kyaaaaaah! They're coming! Do something!

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: Aaaaah! Bring it on! Come at me, you bastards!
Amid the screams, hundreds of soldiers raised their swords and lunged.
The students—whether screaming, crying, or defiant—all shut their eyes tightly.
Light burst from the soldiers' bodies.
Their forms flashed like strobes, then shot skyward toward a single point.

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: W-what…?
Every gaze turned to the rooftop of the central building.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Thank you all for attending our presentation.
The students stared blankly.
They still couldn't grasp the situation.
Teachers rushed into the park, panting.
Faculty members of all levels—lower, upper, and graduating classes—had mobilized.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: You! Get down here right now! What do you think you're doing, turning the school upside down?

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Ah, Professor Siena. Lovely night, isn't it?

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Lovely night my foot! Get down here now, or I'll come up there and kill you!

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: But this is still a presentation. Let us finish before we go.

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: Wait, so it was all fake?

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: We've been tricked! Damn it! I almost had a heart attack!

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: Most were still dazed, but the quicker-witted few pointed and shouted at Shirone's group.

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: You con artists! How dare Class Five pull a stunt like this? This isn't a presentation—it's a scam!

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: They should get zero points for this! Ghosts don't exist! You're finished!

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: The Supernatural Spiritual Science Research Club has proven the existence of ghosts through this presentation. Yet you still refuse to believe.

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: Of course we don't! You can't just make up ghosts and expect us to accept it! You're definitely failing!

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: Yeah! This is just a cheap prank! Anyone could do this! If I wanted to—
A Class Four boy trailed off, blinking.
Others followed suit, murmuring as they looked around.

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: But… how did they even do this? Do you know?

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: No, I can't even begin to guess. It felt so real. And they fooled all 400 of us.

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: Those bastards… what kind of trick did they pull?

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Honestly, even I couldn't yet figure out their methods.
It was impossible to immediately deduce the combination of Neid's engineering, photon emission from the infinite realm, and Iruki's countless equations.

blocks.speaker: Alpheus
blocks.content: Hohohoho. It seems we've been outplayed this time.
The teachers turned.

blocks.speaker: Alpheus
blocks.content: Alpheus laughed heartily as he gazed up at Shirone's group.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Headmaster, this is no laughing matter. Whatever they did, they illegally trespassed into restricted areas. There's evidence of hologram devices—

blocks.speaker: Alpheus
blocks.content: Oh? So even Professor Siena doesn't know?
Had he not specialized in light magic, he too might have missed the information transmission techniques woven into their strategy.

blocks.speaker: Alpheus
blocks.content: But was this truly possible for students?
In a way, it had been a gamble for Shirone's group.

blocks.speaker: Alpheus
blocks.content: Alpheus wanted to praise them for that.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: It's embarrassing, but I don't know yet. However, just because I don't understand the mechanism doesn't mean they've convinced the faculty.

blocks.speaker: Alpheus
blocks.content: Hoho, I misspoke. Being outplayed wasn't what I meant.

blocks.speaker: Alpheus
blocks.content: Then…?
Alpheus held his tongue and looked back at the students.

blocks.speaker: Alpheus
blocks.content: Let's watch for now. The presentation isn't over yet.
